stemHtml: Many aeroplanes are equipped with speed brakes. Speed brakes are usually made up of one or several panels:

options.a: Which extend from the wings upper surface into the airstream to disrupt the laminar flow, reducing lift and increasing induced drag.
options.b: Somewhere on the fuselage or wing which extends into the airstream to create a turbulent airflow and increase parasite drag.
options.c: That form part of the primary flight controls and deflect to generate more downwash which in turn increases induced drag.
options.d: Which extend into the airstream to create vortices that re-energise the boundary layer resulting in an increase in parasite drag.
